title: rosa canina var. subcristata
intro: Rosa canina var. subcristata, also known as the Dwarf Scotch Rose, is a variety of the Rosa canina plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its hips, which are high in vitamins A and C, as well as antioxidants and flavonoids. The hips of the Rosa canina var. subcristata have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall health and well-being.
